spec.goal: Harden same-thread continuation against tracker read-after-write lag without reopening the merged XY-157 lane.
success_criteria: Turn-1 continuation validation accepts a locally successful startup transition even when the immediate tracker reread is stale.
Continuation-blocking local stop writes remain blocking until tracker rereads confirm the issue is active again.
Daemon retry scheduling distinguishes a clean continuation boundary from other successful exits, so completed review handoffs never enqueue continuation retries.
Continuation retry retention matches only the exact original stale startable state instead of any configured startable state.
Targeted tests cover stale reread cases for both startup-transition and stop-write paths.
Repo-native verification passes on the repaired lane.
constraints: Do not rewrite XY-157 history or reopen the merged PR; land this as a follow-up fix lane.
Preserve the conservative terminal-path enforcement added in XY-157.
Do not touch the root checkout's unrelated WORKFLOW.md change.

tasks.title: Pin the stale-reread semantics
tasks.objective: Identify the smallest safe contract for local successful tracker writes versus stale tracker rereads so the repair closes the late review without reopening the old continuation holes.
inputs: Late review threads on PR #18 discussion_r2958265829 and discussion_r2958265833
Current orchestrator continuation guard and tracker tool bridge logic on origin/main
outputs: A concrete repair shape for startup-transition lag and stop-write lag
Target test list for the stale-reread cases
verification: Cross-check the chosen repair against the current code paths and existing continuation tests

tasks.title: Implement lag hardening and tests
tasks.objective: Apply the runtime changes and add targeted tests for stale tracker rereads after local tracker writes.
inputs: Current continuation and tracker bridge tests
outputs: Code changes in the continuation guard / tracker bridge
Targeted tests for stale startup-transition and stop-write rereads
verification: Targeted cargo test coverage for the new stale-reread scenarios

tasks.title: Run gates and record repair evidence
tasks.objective: Run repo-native verification, update the saved plan state/evidence, and determine whether the lane is ready for review.
outputs: Updated plan state reflecting execution results

tasks.title: Separate continuation outcome from generic success
tasks.objective: Carry an explicit continuation outcome signal through the daemon child-exit path so continuation retries are only scheduled for clean continuation boundaries.
inputs: Current RunSummary / child-exit retry scheduling flow
outputs: A dedicated continuation outcome signal used by child-exit retry scheduling
Regression coverage proving review-handoff success does not reopen the same thread
verification: Targeted cargo tests for child-exit continuation scheduling versus completed review handoff

tasks.title: Narrow stale continuation snapshot matching
tasks.objective: Retain continuation retries only when the tracker reread still matches the exact original startable state captured at continuation start.
inputs: Current retry retention policy and queued retry fallback logic
outputs: A persisted exact stale-state token for continuation retries
Regression coverage proving later moves to other startable states do not reopen the old thread
verification: Targeted cargo tests for continuation retry retention across multiple configured startable states

triggers: The repair needs a broader contract change than a local post-merge fix.
Tests show the stale-reread cases require changing the lifecycle or tracker tool surface.
Review feedback implies new module or workflow boundaries.

evidence: 2026-03-19: Created XY-183 as a follow-up fix after PR #18 merged and then received a final Codex review pass at 2026-03-19T07:14:42Z with two new read-after-write lag findings.

2026-03-19: Pinned the repair shape to two local signals: turn-1 continuation now honors a locally successful startup transition when the immediate tracker reread still reflects the pre-write snapshot, and continuation-blocking local stop writes stay blocking until tracker rereads confirm the issue is active again.

2026-03-19: Added stale-reread regression coverage in orchestrator::tests::continuation_guard_allows_turn_one_after_local_startup_transition_on_stale_reread plus tracker_tool_bridge stale-active stop-write tests for both opt-out labels and non-In-Progress transitions.

2026-03-19: Post-commit full-harness verification exposed one remaining shared-state flake in state::tests::shared_dispatch_slots_honor_configured_limit_across_process_local_stores; root cause was failed slot contenders relying on implicit issue-claim guard drop instead of explicit unlock on the false-return path.

2026-03-19: Hardened StateStore shared-lock cleanup by explicitly unlocking failed contender issue-claim guards before returning false and adding deterministic state regressions for failed_shared_slot_attempt_releases_issue_claim_before_retry plus shared_issue_claim_reopens_same_issue_after_clear_across_process_local_stores.

2026-03-19: Codex review on PR #19 identified a daemon-handoff regression: clear_lease must not explicitly unlock handoff-cloned issue or dispatch guards because clone_issue_claim_for_child and clone_dispatch_slot_for_child duplicate those fds into the daemon child.

2026-03-19: Refined the shared-lock repair to tag guards with handoff_cloned metadata so clear_lease explicitly unlocks ordinary guards for deterministic release but preserves handoff-cloned guards until the last inherited holder drops them; failed contender issue claims still unlock immediately on false-return.

2026-03-19: Added adopted_issue_claim_blocks_same_issue_after_parent_clears_local_guard to lock the same-issue handoff case: with two shared slots available, parent clear_lease now drops only its local cloned guard while the adopted child still blocks reacquisition of the same issue claim.

2026-03-19: A subsequent Codex review on PR #19 flagged a stricter safety hole on head `ca93624`: active-start retry/recovery lanes could still let stale active rereads clear local stop writes before the tracker had actually reflected those writes.

2026-03-19: Repaired TrackerToolBridge by restoring the local active-state guard ahead of reread-based continuation unblocking, so local stop writes stay blocking unless the lane still reflects an active local state.

2026-03-19: Replaced the optimistic reactivation tests with active-start stale-reread regressions for both opt-out-label and non-In-Progress stop writes, then reran cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, cargo make test, and git diff --check on the repaired diff.

2026-03-19: A later Codex review on PR #19 flagged that continuation retries still only preserved stale-startable tracker snapshots through turn 1, so long-lived read-after-write lag could fall back to a fresh attempt/run_id instead of a retained continuation retry.

2026-03-19: Extended continuation tolerance across turns and reused `preferred_issue_state = In Progress` as the retry-retention signal so continuation retries still queue and dispatch when the tracker reread remains in a configured startable state.

2026-03-19: Hardened `retry_entry_is_temporarily_blocked()` to apply concurrency gating against the continuation's planned dispatch state rather than the stale tracker state, which keeps stale-startable continuation retries queued when an `In Progress` per-state cap blocks dispatch.

2026-03-19: Added regressions for schedule_retry_after_child_exit_retains_continuation_retry_for_stale_startable_issue, due_continuation_retry_dispatches_when_issue_still_reflects_startable_state, due_continuation_retry_stays_queued_when_stale_startable_issue_hits_in_progress_state_cap, and continuation_guard_allows_turn_two_after_local_startup_transition_on_stale_reread; reran cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, cargo make test, and git diff --check on the repaired diff.

2026-03-19: A later Codex review on PR #19 exposed two deeper contract bugs on head `18a7492`: child-exit retry scheduling still inferred continuation from any successful exit, and stale continuation retention still treated any configured startable state as the original snapshot.
2026-03-19: Reopened execution on XY-183 to separate continuation outcome signaling from generic success and to persist the exact original startable state for stale continuation retry retention.

2026-03-19: Added explicit `continuation_pending` run status plumbing through child-exit retry scheduling so only clean continuation boundaries enqueue `RetryKind::Continuation`; completed successful exits now release their retry claim instead of reopening the same thread.

2026-03-19: Persisted `continuation_initial_issue_state` on queued continuation retries and narrowed stale continuation retention to exact startable-state matches, so a move from `Todo` to another configured startable state like `Backlog` no longer resurrects the old thread.
2026-03-19: Fresh verification on the repaired diff passed: cargo make fmt-check, cargo make lint, cargo make test, git diff --check, plus targeted continuation scheduling/retention regressions.

2026-03-19: Another late Codex review on PR #19 after head `caaa626` exposed two final continuity gaps: startable-start lanes still hard-blocked on local stop writes even when a fresh reread had already reactivated the issue, and continuation retries still overwrote the original startable state when the retried run was prepared from a refreshed `In Progress` snapshot.

2026-03-19: Repaired the remaining gaps by letting startable-start lanes consult a fresh reread before enforcing local stop-write blocking, while keeping active-start lanes conservative, and by persisting `IssueRunPlan.initial_issue_state` so the continuation guard and later retries retain the original startable-state hint even after tracker rereads briefly catch up to `In Progress`.
